assertion_results.text: Investigation identifies two independent root causes: (1) convention reference formatter using wrong case transform, (2) task creation using Feature issue type ID instead of Task (Steps 2-4)
assertion_results.evidence: investigation.md lines 124-130: Root Cause 1 'Convention reference formatter uses wrong case transform' in shared/convention-utils.md. Lines 132-136: Root Cause 2 'Task creation uses Feature issue type ID instead of Task' in plan-feature/SKILL.md Step 6a. Lines 140-147 provide independence assessment confirming different modules, different code paths, independent fixability.

assertion_results.text: Decomposition Guard is triggered because the two root causes are in different modules/code paths (Step 6)
assertion_results.evidence: decomposition-guard.md lines 5-6: 'The investigation (Steps 2-4) identified **two independent root causes** in different modules with different code paths. This triggers the Decomposition Guard per the skill definition.' investigation.md line 147: 'This independence triggers the **Decomposition Guard** (Step 6).'

assertion_results.text: Decomposition Guard presents both issues with their affected files/modules (Step 6)
assertion_results.evidence: decomposition-guard.md lines 11-12: Issue 1 '**Convention reference formatter uses wrong case transform** (in `shared/convention-utils.md`)' with description. Lines 15-16: Issue 2 '**Task creation uses Feature issue type ID instead of Task** (in `plan-feature/SKILL.md` Step 6a)' with description.

assertion_results.text: Decomposition Guard offers the user two options: Proceed with single Task or Split into separate Bugs (Step 6)
assertion_results.evidence: decomposition-guard.md lines 20-24: Options section with '1. **Proceed** -- create a single Task covering both fixes' and '2. **Split** -- I recommend creating separate Bugs for each independent issue, then triaging each one individually' with Bug A and Bug B listed. Ends with 'Choose (1/2):'.

assertion_results.text: The skill does NOT silently create a single Task bundling both fixes — it waits for user input (Step 6 stop rule)
assertion_results.evidence: decomposition-guard.md line 29: 'The skill **stops here and waits for user input**. No Task will be created until the user explicitly chooses an option.' Lines 31-32 explain the two possible paths based on user choice.
